Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the verge of breaking Sachin Tendulkar's 36-year-old India record
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (BCCI Photo)

Sooryavanshi's remarkable rise



A chance to break Sachin Tendulkar 's record



'Words cannot explain it': Sooryavanshi



Will the wait end in Belfast?



Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think young players should get early opportunities in international cricket? Yes, If they earn it. No, Experience should come first. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

India vs Ireland T20Is: Squads

India

Ireland

NEW DELHI: All eyes will be on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi when India take on Ireland in the first T20I of the two-match series in Belfast on Friday.The teenage batting sensation has become one of the biggest talking points in world cricket, with fans eagerly waiting to see if he will make his India debut. If he gets a game, Sooryavanshi will create history by becoming the youngest player to represent India in men's international cricket.Sooryavanshi earned his maiden senior call-up after an impressive IPL 2026 season. Playing for Rajasthan Royals, he scored 776 runs in 16 innings at a staggering strike rate of 237. He also smashed 72 sixes, setting a new record for the most sixes in a single IPL season.The youngster carried his form into the India A tri-series in Sri Lanka. In the one-day final against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla, he blasted 94 off just 29 balls, once again showing why he is regarded as one of the brightest young talents in the game.At 15 years and 71 days, Sooryavanshi became the youngest player ever to be selected in India's senior men's squad, breaking Sachin Tendulkar's 36-year-old record.Tendulkar made his international debut in 1989 against Pakistan at the age of 16 years and 205 days.If Sooryavanshi is included in the playing XI against Ireland, he will surpass the batting legend to become India's youngest men's international cricketer.Speaking after receiving his maiden India call-up, Sooryavanshi described wearing the India jersey as a dream come true."Words cannot explain it. The reason I picked up the bat from day one and went to the ground for practice: that dream is fulfilled now. The biggest step in that journey was completed today. I truly cannot put this feeling into words," he said in a video shared by the BCCI.The teenager admitted he was overwhelmed when he first saw the India jersey."I felt like a dream when I saw that T-shirt; I couldn't stop smiling. Sometimes things happen that you never imagined could happen. When it finally does, you don't know how to react. That was exactly how I felt," he added.Whether he makes his debut on Friday or later in the series, excitement around Sooryavanshi continues to grow. For now, the biggest question ahead of India's clash against Ireland remains the same: Will the 15-year-old get his first India cap?: Shreyas Iyer (captain), Tilak Varma (vice-captain), Ravi Bishnoi, Abhishek Sharma, Suryansh Shedge, Prasidh Krishna, Sanju Samson, Axar Patel, Harshit Rana, Ishan Kishan, Washington Sundar, Arshdeep Singh, Shivam Dube, Prince Yadav, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.: Lorcan Tucker (captain), Ross Adair, Ben Calitz, Gareth Delany, George Dockrell, Stephen Doheny, Matthew Humphreys, Gavin Hoey, Matthew Hollard, Liam McCarthy, Jai Moondra, Harry Tector, Tim Tector, Reuben Wilson.
Article Summary: India take on Ireland in the first T20I of the two-match series in Belfast on Friday. If he gets a game, Sooryavanshi will become the youngest player to represent India in men's international cricket. The 15-year-old earned his maiden senior call-up after an impressive IPL 2026 season.

Article Title: Minds that move mountains
Article Text:
Thirukkural

with the Times explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text ‘Thirukkural’. Written by Tamil poet and philosopher

Thiruvalluvar

, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each. This text is divided into three books with teachings on virtue, wealth, and love and is considered one of the great works ever on ethics and morality. The Kural has influenced scholars and leaders across social, political, and philosophical spheres.

Motivational speaker, author and diversity champion

Bharathi Bhaskar

explores the masterpiece.

Mt. Fuji may be visible if the weather permits

"O white-faced wonder, O matchless sight, O sublimity, O beauty... As one in a dream of a poem, we being around thee forget to die."

Zenjō

"When the sky is clear, Fuji appears."

Shikantaza

just sitting

"Nilayin Thiriyaadhu Adangiyaan Thotram

Malayinum Maanap Peridhu."

"More imposing than a mountain is the stature of one who remains steadfast and self-controlled."

(Thirukkural 124)

"A wise man climbs Mt. Fuji once; only a fool climbs it twice."

Last week, around this time, I was gazing at Mt. Fuji, Japan. The itinerary for our first day in Tokyo promised visits to a couple of lakes from where, it cautiously said,Our team leader, Murali, had other plans. "Let's go to the foot of Fuji," he insisted to the tour organiser. The guide smiled politely and said, "You cannot always see Fujisan," he said. "Only if you are lucky.” We insisted and the little detour began.The bus which had echoed with songs, jokes and endless chatter suddenly fell silent.As the bus crossed a bend, the guide said over the microphone. "Look to your right. Sometimes she disappears the very next minute."There she was.No mist. No clouds. Fujisan stood before us, radiant against the blue sky, as though she had chosen to welcome our little group. We were among the fortunate ones.Seeing Mt. Fuji had long been a dream. Japanese poetry has celebrated this mountain for centuries—not merely for its flawless shape, but for the serenity and sacredness it radiates. The poet Yone Noguchi captured the feeling beautifully:For centuries, climbing Mt. Fuji was never considered just a trek. It was a spiritual discipline known as. Zen monks ascended the mountain as a moving meditation. The physical exhaustion, followed by the breathtaking vision of Fuji glowing in the morning sun, became a metaphor for emptying the ego before attaining enlightenment. A cherished Zen saying reminds us,It is not merely about the weather. A quiet, uncluttered mind reflects the truth.Standing at the foot of Japan's tallest mountain, a semi-dormant volcano, I found myself thinking of another Zen practice inspired by these mountains—, literally, "just sitting."In a world that glorifies constant action,sounds almost absurd. Yet it is one of the deepest forms of meditation. It teaches us to remain fully present without resistance. When our minds are restless and life is turbulent, there is immense strength in simply being still. Acceptance is not surrender to defeat; it is surrender to reality. And from that acceptance emerges the strength to observe, understand and move forward.Perhaps that is what Fuji stands for.As we walked back to the bus, we spotted little shops displaying paintings of the mountain. Japanese artists often layer delicate washes of water over their canvases, allowing light as if it is imprisoned within the colours. That subtle translucence mirrors Japanese culture — gentle, understated and profound.Yet history reminds us of another Japan. The same civilisation capable of such exquisite refinement also unleashed unimaginable brutality across Asia during the World War II —from the massacres in Singapore to the sufferings inflicted on prisoners forced to build the Siam-Burma death track. Like Fuji herself, beneath breathtaking beauty rests the silent fire of a volcano.As the bus prepared to leave, I took one last look at the serene mountain. The Zen masters say, "Conquering Mt. Fuji is admirable; conquering yourself is far greater”. I thought, every mountain offers the same illumination. Vallular too echoes this:I bowed instinctively before Fujisan, just as the Japanese do.One person in our group said – ‘Let’s see Mt Fuji tomorrow as well’. Our guide smiled and shared another proverb.Our laughter filled the bus. But somewhere behind us stood Fuji — silent, unmoved, and still teaching.
Article Summary: Bharathi Bhaskar explores real-world lessons from the classic Tamil text ‘Thirukkural’ Written by Tamil poet and philosopher, the Kural consists of 1,330 short couplets of seven words each. This text is divided into three books with teachings on virtue, wealth, and love.

Article Title: 'None of our MPs will split': Sharad Pawar's bold assertion in season of defections
Article Text:
Sharad Pawar has asserted that Nationalist Congress Party (SP) will remain united

The defection claim

A small party but a big risk

The NCP reunion that never happened

Could history repeat?

NEW DELHI: "None of our MPs will split. Not a single one of our MLAs is going anywhere … " this was Nationalist Congress Party (SP) chief Sharad Pawar ’s confident, if not bold, assertion that his party remains united and will not tread the same path as Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena (UBT) or Mamata Banerjee’s Trinamool Congress.This comes in response to a claim by NCP MLA Dharmarao Atram that five of the eight MPs of Sharad Pawar's party are ready to cross over to the Nationalist Congress Party led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Sunetra Pawar by December.Pawar’s daughter Supriya Sule , who is a Lok Sabha member from Baramati and one of the eight party MPs, rejected the NCP MLA’s claim and dared him to disclose the names. "Atram might be knowing more. I do not have any idea. We eight MPs were united yesterday, are united today and will remain united tomorrow. If Baba Atram has a list of five MPs, he should disclose the names," Sule said.Sule also took a dig at Atram and quipped: "If he has a list of five MPs, why is he not poaching me? Why is he sparing me?"Sharad Pawar’s NCP-SP has at present 8 MPs and 10 MLAs - making the party vulnerable to poaching. Especially, at a time when at least two regional heavyweights, despite their tall claims of unity, have been dealt a crushing blow by the rebels.The Anti-defection Act, which provides parties a protective shield against defections, failed in both the cases as the rebels played the numbers game with perfection. The Anti-defection Act does not come into play when two-thirds of the legislators decide to change sides together. And that is what happened with both Shiv Sena (UBT) and the Trinamool Congress.In the case of Sharad Pawar’s party - a legally viable defection will require at least 6 MPs to quit together while in the case of MLAs the number would be 7.Sharad Pawar, who played a key role in the formation of the Maha Vikas Aghadi in 2019 to keep the BJP out of power in Maharashtra, has already lost control of his party and symbol to a revolt led by his nephew late Ajit Pawar in 2023. 40 out of the 55 NCP MLAs then sided with Ajit as a helpless Sharad Pawar could do little to save his party.There were moves in 2024 to reunite the two factions of the NCP with both Ajit Pawar and Sharad Pawar striking conciliatory tones. However, the proposed unification talks hit a dead end following Ajit Pawar’s sudden death in January 2026.While both Pawar and Sule have ruled out any rebellion, they would be wary given the recent developments in their state which saw Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena(UBT) suffer a second rebellion.Uddhav lost 6 party MPs to the Eknath Shinde led Shiv Sena four years after the first rebellion by party MLAs that dethroned him from the post of chief minister.Sule reacted sharply to the defections and said: "I honestly wonder if democracy has ceased to exist in this country. Breaking homes and splitting parties has become the norm. These MPs were elected just two and a half years ago, and the next general elections are scheduled for 2029. What was the absolute hurry?"Sule reminded the ruling party of democratic traditions, pointing out that opposition parties like Congress have historically offered unanimous support to the government on crucial legislative matters -- such as the passage of the GST bill -- without requiring political coercion. "What is the need for such vandalism and destruction of parties? If there is no opposition left and everyone joins the government, who will speak for the common citizens?" she asked.The NCP, which is now led by Ajit Pawar’s wife Sunetra Pawar, has just one member in Lok Sabha at present. It may not be averse to increasing its tally for a greater say in the ruling NDA. Both Uddhav Thackeray and Mamata Banerjee had strongly denied reports of any rebellion even as their legislators were plotting a coup. In fact, the other big rebellion this season, which happened in AAP and was the first in the latest series, had also come as a surprise to Arvind Kejriwal and other senior leaders of the party. So, while Sharad Pawar’s confidence is welcome, he needs to be on guard.With NCP MLA Dharmarao Atram setting a December deadline, the fear of defections would be hanging like a sword of Damocles on Sharad Pawar and his daughter Supriya Sule.
Article Summary: Sharad Pawar has asserted that Nationalist Congress Party (SP) will remain united. NCP-SP has at present 8 MPs and 10 MLAs - making the party vulnerable to poaching. Uddhav Thackeray’s Shiv Sena(UBT) suffer a second rebellion.

The 2026 NHL Draft officially began Friday night at KeyBank Center in Buffalo, New York, with blockbuster trades already reshaping the first round before the opening selection. The Toronto Maple Leafs entered the night holding the No. 1 overall pick and selected Gavin McKenna, while several clubs made aggressive moves up and down the board in one of the busiest opening rounds in recent memory.Round 1 started at 7 p.m. ET on Friday, June 26, with Rounds 2 through 7 scheduled for 11 a.m. ET on Saturday, June 27. Fans in the United States can watch on ESPN, NHL Network and ESPN+, while Sportsnet carries the draft in Canada. With multiple first-round trades, surprise selections and several highly regarded prospects still available, the draft remains one of the biggest events of the NHL offseason.Several draft-day trades altered the original first-round lineup, creating significant movement across the board. Below is the final first-round selection order.The opening round featured expected selections at the top, but plenty of movement followed as teams targeted defensemen and centers.Toronto selected Gavin McKenna first overall, while San Jose, Vancouver and Buffalo rounded out the top four.After the first 30 selections, several notable prospects remained available heading into the remainder of the draft:The remaining rounds continue Saturday, beginning at 11 a.m. ET.Buffalo remains the center of attention heading into Day 2 after an opening night filled with trades involving the San Jose Sharks, St. Louis Blues, Anaheim Ducks, Vegas Golden Knights and Montreal Canadiens. While Gavin McKenna headlines the 2026 class, teams will continue searching for value throughout the remaining six rounds as several highly regarded prospects are still waiting to hear their names called.
Article Summary: The 2026 NHL Draft will take place on June 26, 2026. The order of the first- round picks is as follows: Toronto Maple Leafs, San Jose Sharks, New Jersey Devils, Calgary Flames, Washington Capitals, Anaheim Ducks, Pittsburgh Penguins, Ottawa Senators, Montreal Canadiens, New York Islanders.

Caitlin Clark injury update took another disappointing turn Friday after Indiana Fever coach Stephanie White confirmed the All-Star guard will miss Saturday's home game against the Los Angeles Sparks because of a lingering back injury. The setback comes two days after Clark exited the Fever's loss to the Phoenix Mercury in the third quarter and did not return.Clark's absence is significant for an Indiana team trying to stay on track during the middle of the WNBA season. While White expressed optimism about Clark's recovery, she declined to provide a timetable, making the upcoming week-long break a crucial window for the franchise's leading playmaker.White struck a reassuring tone despite confirming Clark would sit out Saturday's contest."Obviously, it's a good time (for Clark to recover) because we have all week next week (without games)," White said, via The Athletic . "So we'll take all week next week just to get her treatment, get her healthy, get her back on the floor and see what happens. … Long-term health and wellness is the most important thing."The coach added that Clark is "OK," but admitted she had "no indication" how long the injury could keep her sidelined.Clark has battled back discomfort throughout the season. She missed Indiana's May 20 victory over the Portland Fire because of a sore back and has appeared on the injury report as probable before multiple games. She also revealed earlier this season that she had been receiving treatment throughout the year."I don't think it necessarily started at a certain point," Clark said in May. "It's just been over the course of the last year when I've kind of been understanding my body more. … Still working through that, but I don't think it's anything of concern."Wednesday's loss to Phoenix raised fresh concerns after Clark aggravated the injury during the second half. The incident came after a physical sequence involving Mercury veteran Alyssa Thomas, whose contact to Clark's throat during a loose-ball scramble was later upgraded by the WNBA to a Flagrant 2 foul. Thomas received the first suspension of her 13-year career.White criticized officials for missing the play in real time."I was more surprised that it wasn't seen by the officials in real time," White said. "… Sometimes I feel like all of the officials are watching the ball, and no one is watching the play. We have a player that's down on the ground. Certainly, some things are going to happen. So I was very disappointed that they didn't see that in real time."At this stage, there is no confirmed return date.The timing of the Fever's schedule could work in Clark's favor. Following Saturday's matchup against Los Angeles, Indiana will not play again until July 5 against the Las Vegas Aces, giving Clark more than a week to recover.White emphasized that the organization will not rush its franchise guard back onto the floor."Again, we want her to be healthy, physically, mentally, emotionally, all of the things," White said. "It's important to remember, again, when you've gone through injury and the traumatic aspect of injury, especially that she had last year, it's ups and downs. … Making sure that she's 100 percent ready to go is the most important thing, and her taking care of that is (a) priority for us."In Clark's absence, veteran Tyasha Harris is expected to move into the starting lineup, while Kelsey Mitchell and rookie Raven Johnson will shoulder additional ballhandling responsibilities.Clark has still produced at an elite level despite managing the injury, averaging 21.2 points, 8.2 assists and 4.0 rebounds across 17 games this season.The extended break gives Indiana its best opportunity yet to get Clark healthy before a demanding stretch featuring two meetings with the Aces and another matchup against the Mercury, a team that handed the Fever one of their toughest losses of the season.
Article Summary: Caitlin Clark will miss Saturday's home game against the Los Angeles Sparks. All-Star guard has been sidelined with a lingering back injury. Coach Stephanie White says she has "no indication" how long the injury could keep her sidelined. Clark is averaging 21.2 points, 8.2 assists and 4.0 rebounds.

Article Title: How to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite
Article Text:
Image via Fortnite

How to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite



Is there a guaranteed way to get the Grim Reaper Sprite in Fortnite?



What does the Grim Reaper Sprite do in Fortnite?



With five brand new Sprites arriving in Fortnite's v41.10 update, the Shattered Coast is filled with more content when it comes to Sprite hunting. Among all the newcomers introduced as part of the Fortnite Gone Wild event, the Grim Reaper Sprite has immediately established itself as one of the most coveted.It is also among the most difficult ones to add to your collection, making it a hot topic within the Fortnite community. If you are looking for more details about finding it, you are at the right place.The section below guides you on obtaining the Grim Reaper Sprite.The Grim Reaper Sprite is a Mythic Sprite, so it’s very rare and hard to get in Fortnite. There’s no guaranteed way to get it, and no location on the map where it reliably spawns, so it’s a true test of patience and persistence.Speaking of where to find it, the Grim Reaper Sprite can be found in Sprite Chests, regular chests, rare chests, golden fishing spots, and as floor loot around the Fortnite island. Of these methods, Sprite Chests are still the best and most reliable method as they’re the only source guaranteed to have at least one Sprite in each opening.Your best bet for finding a Grim Reaper Sprite is opening up as many Sprite Chests as you can find.Vaults are great spots to visit for them, as these treasure troves usually have at least two Sprite Chests available to open. Prioritising Vault locations at the start of a match is one of the most efficient strategies for grinding rare Sprite drops.Fishing out of gold pools around the island is another great method. These drop a variety of loot, and do not always contain Sprites, but when they do, they are oftentimes really rare and unique ones.The answer to this question is no, as the Grim Sprite does not have a guaranteed spawn location for the Grim Sprite in Fortnite. Instead, it is a random loot drop that can appear anywhere around the island, which means luck and random chance play a massive role in how easy or hard it is to find.If finding one through natural loot is proving frustratingly tiring, keep an eye out for players willing to trade. The Grim Reaper Sprite's utility in combat makes it one of the most valuable trade commodities currently in Chapter 7 Season 3.When equipped, the Grim Reaper Sprite marks anyone who attacks you for a short period of time. This makes it an outstanding combat tool, i.e., if an opponent lands a hit on you and tries to disappear into cover or flee across the map, the mark reveals their position, making it significantly harder for them to escape follow-up damage. For aggressive players who frequently find themselves in high-stakes duels, the Grim Reaper Sprite is one of the most impactful Mythic Sprites currently in the game.This was all you needed to know about the Grim Reaper Sprite.
Article Summary: The Grim Reaper Sprite is a Mythic Sprite, so it’s very rare and hard to get in Fortnite. There's no guaranteed way to get it, and no location on the map where it reliably spawns. It can be found in Sprite Chests, regular chests, rare chests, golden fishing spots, and as floor loot around the island.

Article Title: Cape Verde make World Cup history, joining only three nations ever to pull off rare qualification milestone
Article Text:
Cape Verde players celebrate their 0-0 draw with Saudi Arabia after the World Cup Group H soccer match in Houston, Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/David J. Phillip)

Three draws, one historic qualification



Cape Verde players celebrate after the World Cup Group H soccer match between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia in Houston, Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)

A feat achieved by only three nations

Wales (1958)

Republic of Ireland (1990)

Cape Verde (2026)

Vozinha delivers another heroic performance

Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha (1) celebrates after the World Cup Group H soccer match between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia in Houston, Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)

Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha (1) makes a save during the World Cup Group H soccer match between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia in Houston, Friday, June 26, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)



From outsiders to World Cup giant-killers

Cape Verde's remarkable FIFA World Cup 2026 debut reached another historic milestone on Friday as the Blue Sharks secured qualification for the Round of 32 despite drawing all three of their group-stage matches. A disciplined 0-0 draw against Saudi Arabia, combined with Spain's 1-0 victory over Uruguay, was enough to send the African newcomers through in second place behind Group H winners Spain.The achievement completes one of the tournament's most improbable stories. Competing at their first-ever World Cup, the island nation of just over 500,000 people progressed from a group featuring former world champions Spain and Uruguay, becoming the smallest country ever to reach the knockout stages of a men's FIFA World Cup.Cape Verde entered their final Group H fixture knowing qualification remained within reach, but their fate would depend on events unfolding simultaneously in Guadalajara.The Blue Sharks had already exceeded expectations by frustrating Spain in a goalless draw before recovering from behind to earn an impressive 2-2 draw against Uruguay. Another point against Saudi Arabia would leave them on three points, but progression depended on Spain avoiding defeat against Marcelo Bielsa's side.Cape Verde delivered another disciplined defensive performance, holding Saudi Arabia to a 0-0 draw while nervously awaiting news from the other match. Moments after the final whistle, confirmation arrived that Spain had defeated Uruguay 1-0. The result sparked emotional celebrations among Cape Verde's players, coaching staff and supporters, many of whom were seen embracing and shedding tears as qualification was officially secured.The reward for their extraordinary group-stage campaign is a Round of 32 meeting with defending world champions Argentina in Miami on July 3.By drawing each of their three group-stage matches and still progressing, Cape Verde joined one of the most exclusive statistical clubs in World Cup history.They became only the fourth nation to qualify for the knockout rounds after drawing all three group-stage matches. Before Cape Verde, the feat had been achieved only by:Cameroon also drew all three matches during their World Cup debut in 1982 but failed to progress, while New Zealand suffered the same fate after recording three draws during what was their second appearance at the 2010 World Cup.Cape Verde's achievement extends beyond that unique record. They are also the first nation to progress from the group stage in their debut World Cup appearance since Slovakia accomplished the feat in 2010, and the first debutant to go unbeaten throughout the group stage since Senegal's memorable run in 2002.Much of Cape Verde's success has been built on the remarkable performances of veteran goalkeeper Vozinha, whose tournament has transformed him into one of the breakout stars of the World Cup.The 40-year-old produced another outstanding display against Saudi Arabia to preserve the clean sheet. He first denied Mohamed Kanno in first-half stoppage time by comfortably gathering a dangerous header before producing an excellent diving save in the 66th minute to push away Mohammed Abu Al-Shamat's effort. Deep into stoppage time, with qualification hanging in the balance, he made one final crucial stop to deny Abdullah Al-Hamdan in the 92nd minute.The veteran's performances throughout the tournament have earned global attention, helping him amass more than 16 million Instagram followers during Cape Verde's historic campaign.Watching proudly from a luxury suite was his mother, Ana Candida Évora, who waved a small Cape Verde flag throughout the match. It was only her second appearance at the tournament after visa issues prevented her from witnessing her son's unforgettable seven-save display against Spain earlier in the group stage.Few gave Cape Verde any realistic chance of surviving a group containing Spain, Uruguay and Saudi Arabia before the tournament began. Instead, the Blue Sharks leave the opening phase unbeaten after frustrating one former world champion and denying another, while becoming one of the biggest stories of the competition.Spain finished top of Group H with seven points following victories over Saudi Arabia and Uruguay after their opening draw against Cape Verde. The Blue Sharks claimed second place with three points, edging past Uruguay and Saudi Arabia to continue their historic adventure.Their next challenge will be the reigning world champions Argentina. On paper, Lionel Messi's side will start as overwhelming favourites, but Cape Verde have already demonstrated throughout this tournament that reputation alone means very little once the match begins.
Article Summary: Cape Verde are the fourth nation to qualify for the knockout rounds after drawing all three of their group-stage matches. The Blue Sharks are the smallest country ever to reach the knockout stages of a men's FIFA World Cup. Cape Verde will face defending world champions Argentina in Miami on July 3.

Article Title: Nick Bosa’s brother Joey Bosa could retire following no team interest in free agency market
Article Text:
Nick Bosa’s brother Joey Bosa could retire following no team interest in free agency market (Image via Getty: Nick Bosa and Joey Bosa)

Buffalo Bills DE and Nick Bosa ’s older brother Joey Bosa is projected to retire at 30 after no free agency signing

Cheryl Bosa wanted Joey Bosa and Nick Bosa to play for San Francisco 49ers

When does age catch up to NFL players?

After several reporters and analysts predicted Buffalo Bills dynamite defensive end Joey Bosa for several NFL teams, and mainly the San Francisco 49ers,it has come to notice that he may retire rather than return to the gridiron for the upcoming 2026 NFL season.ESPN NFL Insider Adam Schefter said, “It is more likely than not that Joey Bosa has played his last NFL down.”NFL Insider Adam Schefter further said that Joey Bosa may not sign with any NFL team before Week 1 of the 2026 season.“Could a situation like the [San Francisco] 49ers come along that entices him enough to come out and play again? Yeah, absolutely!We saw it happen last year with [Indianapolis Colts QB] Philip Rivers, so you never know when a player is fully done and when he’s not done.But the fact of the matter is, I think if the Niners wanted to pair those two together, it probably would have happened already.I think if Joey Bosa wanted to play football, it would have happened already,” Adam Schefter said.The Buffalo Bills pass rusher was linked to several teams, like the Tennessee Titans, Green Bay Packers, Dallas Cowboys, and Seattle Seahawks, but none of those connections came to fruition.Despite having impeccable numbers throughout his 10-year-old NFL career, he remains a free agent in the 2026 offseason.The 30-year-old defensive end recorded 372 total tackles [269 solo, 103 assisted], 77.0 sacks, 22 forced fumbles, 4 passes defended, and 4 fumble recoveries in 122 NFL games.In the 2025 NFL season, Joey Bosa recorded 29 total tackles, 5.0 sacks, 4 forced fumbles, and 2 passes defended in 15 NFL games.Even Joey Bosa and Nick Bosa’s mother, Cheryl Bosa, once posted a photo on her Instagram Story: two brothers clad in San Francisco 49ers gear.She would have wanted her sons to represent one team and not separate teams. Something that Donna Kelce once had to face:Her two popular NFL sons – Jason Kelce and Travis Kelce – faced each other four times on the gridiron.Brothers who grew up playing together had to play on opposing teams. And their dear mother?She resorted to wearing a jacket with half the Philadelphia Eagles jersey [Jason Kelce’s team] and half the Kansas City Chiefs jersey [Travis Kelce’s team].But, but, but. Who is paying heed? Now that the NFL Insider has said it. Now that there’s nothing happening in the San Francisco 49ers universe.Now that the Bosa brothers may not wear the same uniform. Now that former Buffalo Bills defensive end Joey Bosa is predicted to retire…We still have 74 days until the 2026 NFL season begins! Let’s hold our breaths…for the Bosa Brothers’ reunion…Bleacher Report’s Ty Schalter reported in June 2018 that some players seem to turn from stars to has-beens overnight, while some walk away from the game with their powers intact.But when does the age catch up to NFL players? In particular, defensive linemen [now that we have Joey Bosa’s return/retirement in question].Bleacher Report’s Ty Schlater reports that a defensive lineman’s seventh year in the NFL marks the line. After that, his time is very, very short.There is a significant drop-off in the number of qualifying starters from Year 7 to Year 8 and from Year 9 to Year 10.The decline flattens for a while, as the best defensive linemen can stick around a very long time. But the quick development, long plateau, and quick decline are obvious.Joey Bosa has played for 10 long NFL seasons… Is his retirement almost here? Only time will tell…
Article Summary: Joey Bosa may retire rather than return to the gridiron for the upcoming 2026 NFL season. ESPN NFL Insider Adam Schefter said, “It is more likely than not that Joey Bosa has played his last NFL down.” Bosa recorded 372 total tackles [269 solo, 103 assisted], 77.0 sacks, 22 forced fumbles, 4 passes defended, and 4 fumble recoveries in 122 games.

Article Title: Cruz Beckham and Jackie Apostel spark fresh engagement buzz after hidden ring detail steals spotlight at FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
Cruz Beckham and Jackie Apostel (Image Via Getty)

Cruz Beckham and Jackie Apostel fuel engagement talk during Brazil’s World Cup match

“Juuuust happy to be here.”

Cruz Beckham and Jackie Apostel have spoken openly about how their relationship began

Cruz Beckham and his girlfriend Jackie Apostel have sparked engagement rumours after fans noticed a ring on her finger during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The couple attended Brazil’s match against Scotland at Miami Stadium on Wednesday with Cruz’s father, David Beckham. Jackie later shared photos from the game on Instagram, where the ring caught people’s attention. Neither Cruz nor Jackie has confirmed an engagement, and there has been no official statement from their representatives.Jackie and Cruz both wore Brazil jerseys as they watched Brazil beat Scotland 3-0. Jackie, who was born in São Paulo to a Brazilian mother and a German father, has often spoken about her connection to Brazil.She shared pictures from the stadium on Instagram and wrote,In the photos, fans quickly noticed a ring on her finger, leading to fresh engagement rumours online. However, the couple has not commented on the speculation.David Beckham was also seen enjoying the match. Although he earned 115 caps for England during his football career, he appeared to celebrate Brazil’s win with his son. The pair shared a warm hug in the stands, while Jackie also wore one of Romeo Beckham’s INTRA sportswear designs during the outing.According to the Daily Mail, the newspaper has contacted Cruz’s representatives for comment, but no response has been made public.The couple have been together since last summer after being introduced through their managers. Both share a strong interest in music, and Jackie has become an important part of Cruz’s creative journey.During a recent fan Q&A, Jackie explained that they first met during a songwriting session.“We got put in a writing session and just like both were so obsessed with what we were doing and music. We became really good friends and then here we are!”When asked about their first date, the couple laughed while trying to remember the details. Cruz first thought it was a rooftop cinema, before changing his answer.“So me and Cruz went to the studio and we sat in the studio until 3am, but like you didn’t even kiss me!” Jackie said.Cruz replied, “I was nervous.”Jackie added that they both already knew there was something special between them.“To be fair we knew though, it felt different from other sessions. And then the next day we went to a rooftop cinema with friend Stella who third-wheeled us. And that was our first real date.”Jackie has also helped Cruz with writing songs and promoting his music. Cruz released his debut single in 2025 and has continued working on his music career.
Article Summary: Cruz Beckham and Jackie Apostel have sparked engagement rumours. Fans noticed a ring on her finger during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The couple attended Brazil’s match against Scotland at Miami Stadium. Neither Cruz nor Jackie has confirmed an engagement and there has been no official statement from their representatives.

Article Title: Rangers trade Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau
Article Text:
Brett Berard skates before a March 16 game against the Edmonton Oilers at Madison Square Garden. Mandatory Credit: Danny Wild-Imagn Images (Image via Getty)

Brett Berard gets the fresh start that had already started to feel inevitable

William Trudeau gives the Rangers a specific answer, not a headline trade

The New York Rangers did not make the blockbuster trade many fans had been waiting for before the 2026 NHL Draft. They still made a move that says plenty about where Brett Berard stood in their plans.The Rangers announced Friday, June 26, that president and general manager Chris Drury acquired defenseman William Trudeau from the Montreal Canadiens in exchange for Berard. Canadiens general manager Kent Hughes also confirmed the deal through Canadiens PR, sending Berard to an Eastern Conference rival after his role in New York had clearly tightened.Berard, 23, was once an interesting piece in the Rangers’ forward pipeline. The fit had changed by the time this trade arrived.According to the New York Post, Berard was looking for a change of scenery after a difficult 2025-26 season. That part matters because he was also no longer waiver-exempt. If New York did not see a clear roster path for him, keeping him into training camp carried risk. A demotion could have exposed him to waivers, where the Rangers might have lost him for nothing.His NHL usage also told the story. Berard played 35 games for the Rangers in 2024-25 and recorded six goals and four assists.This past season, he went scoreless in 13 games with New York.That is not enough runway for a young forward trying to establish himself. It is also not enough production for a team that already had several bottom-six forward options fighting for space.Montreal gives Berard something New York no longer seemed able to offer: a cleaner opening. The Canadiens get a 23-year-old winger with NHL experience, restricted free agent status, and a real reason to see if a reset changes the picture.For Berard, this is not just a trade. It is a way out of a crowded room.Trudeau is not the kind of return that makes a fan base stop scrolling. That does not make the deal empty.The 23-year-old defenseman is 6’1” and 205 pounds. He spent the 2025-26 season with the Laval Rocket in the AHL, where he had eight goals and 12 assists in 62 games. The Rangers noted that Trudeau ranked third among Rocket defensemen in goals and fourth in assists and points.Across four AHL seasons, all with Laval, Trudeau played 260 games and recorded 28 goals and 62 assists for 90 points. He also appeared in 15 postseason games.The roster logic is easy to see. New York moved a forward whose path had narrowed and added a left-shot defenseman to a pipeline that needed more balance. Trudeau was originally selected by Montreal in the fourth round, No. 113 overall, of the 2021 NHL Draft.This trade will not fix the Rangers’ blue line by itself. It was never going to. The value is in timing and asset management.Drury moved Berard before the waiver question could become a bigger problem. Montreal took the swing on a young forward who needed a new setup. New York got a defenseman with AHL production and a clearer organizational fit.No one won the offseason with this move. Both teams solved a real problem before it got louder.
Article Summary: The New York Rangers traded Brett Berard to the Montreal Canadiens for William Trudeau. Berard, 23, played 35 games for the Rangers in 2024-25 and recorded six goals and four assists. Trudeau played 260 games and recorded 28 goals and 62 assists for 90 points in the AHL.

Article Title: Former TNA and All Japan champion Joe Doering dies at 44 after long brain cancer battle
Article Text:
Joe Doering (Image Via Getty)

Joe Doering’s wrestling career and success in TNA and All Japan Pro Wrestling

Joe Doering fought brain cancer for nearly a decade



Former TNA and All Japan Pro Wrestling star Joe Doering has died at the age of 44 after a long fight with brain cancer. Maple Leaf Pro Wrestling confirmed the news on Friday, June 26, saying Doering passed away peacefully with his family by his side. He is survived by his wife, Lindsay. The wrestling world is now remembering him not only for his success in the ring but also for the strength he showed throughout his illness. The news was first shared by Maple Leaf Pro Wrestling through its official statement.Joe Doering began his professional wrestling career in 2004 and quickly built a name for himself in Japan and North America. He became one of All Japan Pro Wrestling’s top stars, winning the Triple Crown Heavyweight Championship twice.He also captured the World Tag Team Championship four times with four different partners, making him one of the promotion’s most respected heavyweights.In TNA, Doering first appeared in 2005 before returning years later for the biggest run of his career. He joined Eric Young and Cody Deaner in the Violent By Design faction, where the group won the TNA World Tag Team Championship twice under the Freebird Rule.He also challenged Josh Alexander for the TNA World Championship at Against All Odds in July 2022, but Alexander successfully kept the title.That turned out to be Doering’s only shot at the company’s top championship.Doering’s fight with brain cancer began in 2016 when doctors found his first brain tumor. He underwent surgery and returned to wrestling, even winning another Triple Crown title after his recovery. In 2022, the tumor came back, forcing him to step away from TNA for another surgery.His family later revealed that he had been diagnosed with a third brain tumor in late 2025. Earlier this week, they shared that he had entered hospice care before his passing on Friday.During his career, Doering shared the ring with several well-known names, including Hiroshi Tanahashi, Tommy Dreamer, Jun Akiyama and many of Japan’s biggest wrestling stars. As of now, TNA has not announced whether it will honor him during this weekend’s Slammiversary pay-per-view.
Article Summary: Joe Doering has died at the age of 44 after a long fight with brain cancer. Maple Leaf Pro Wrestling confirmed the news on Friday, June 26, saying Doering passed away peacefully with his family by his side. The wrestling world is now remembering him not only for his success in the ring but also for the strength he showed throughout his illness.

Article Title: What is Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller’s theory on Auston Matthews production dip?
Article Text:
Auston Matthews’ injury has fueled speculation about his playoff absence, indicating trade moves. (Image Via Twitter)

Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller cites a reason why Auston Matthews’ production has dipped

Toronto Maple Leafs believe Jim Hiller has been the right choice for being a head coach

The Toronto Maple Leafs' decision to appoint Jim Hiller as the franchise's 41st head coach of the team may have surprised many, but the coach has begun analyzing the players and the team's performance over the years. The coach has recently analyzed Auston Matthews' performance over the years and believes that his production has dipped significantly over the years. Matthews was drafted by the Leafs No. 1 overall in the 2016 NHL Draft and spent his first three seasons playing under Hiller, and it is to be noted that Hiller has served as an assistant coach for the Leafs for four years from 2015 until 2019. Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller has recently analyzed that team's performance and has provided a significant reason for believing Auston Matthews' recent dip in goal production. Hiller, who was officially introduced as the Maple Leafs' head coach this week, is no stranger to Matthews. The 57-year-old spent four seasons as an assistant coach in Toronto from 2015 to 2019, overlapping with the early years of Matthews' NHL career after the forward was selected first overall in the 2016 NHL Draft.Though Matthews has established himself as one of the NHL's elite goal scorers, Jim Hiller has cited reasons why Auston Matthews’ production has dipped significantly. According to Hiller, the reason for Auston Matthews recent dip in goal production may have to do with the team's power play rather than the star center's individual performance.While addressing the media during a press conference, Hiller stated, “There are a lot of things there.One thing — Auston was always a really dangerous player on the power play. I don’t think they’ve been as dangerous recently. I think that is one area."He further said, “Of course, there is five-on-five, and him being completely healthy, and those types of things. But I hope and envision we can get our power play going a little bit more. For the top guys, when they get some good momentum on the power play, it generally spreads out to the five-on-five game. That will be a real focus.”Rather than pointing to the forward's individual play, the new coach suggested the Maple Leafs' struggling power play has played a major role.Besides, Matthews has been away from the team for a prolonged period due to his Grade 3 MCL tear and quad contusion, which he sustained in a knee-on-knee collision with Anaheim Ducks defenseman Radko Gudas. The Toronto Maple Leafs captain has recently undergone surgery as part of his recovery. However, he plans to return to the team very soon.The Toronto Maple Leafs have named Jim Hiller as the franchise's 41st head coach, completing a major responsibility ahead of the 2026 NHL Draft. Hiller, as a coach, has a wide range of experience as he has most recently coached the Los Angeles Kings, compiling a 93-58-24 record across parts of three seasons before being dismissed in March. Hiller was praised for his leadership qualities, extensive coaching experience, and his ability to connect well with players. Despite his departure years ago, the Maple Leafs remained impressed with his growth as a coach and his evolving leadership style.
Article Summary: Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller cites a reason why Auston Matthews’ production has dipped over the years. Hiller spent four seasons as an assistant coach in Toronto from 2015 to 2019, overlapping with the early years of Matthews' NHL career. The 57-year-old has most recently coached the Los Angeles Kings, compiling a 93-58-24 record across parts of three seasons.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Dallas Cowboys linked to $100 Million Las Vegas Raiders star in blockbuster three-team deal with Tennessee Titans
Article Text:
The Dallas Cowboys have been linked to a proposed three-team trade that would bring a $96 million Raiders star to Dallas while sending George Pickens to the Tennessee Titans. The idea, floated by Bleacher Report's Kristopher Knox, follows reports that the Cowboys previously made multiple offers for the Raiders standout. While the trade remains hypothetical, Dallas' long-standing interest and Pickens' uncertain contract situation continue to fuel speculation.





Category Details Player Maxx Crosby Current Team Las Vegas Raiders Rumored Team Dallas Cowboys Contract Status Under contract Salary Cap Hit Part of four-year, $96 million extension Trade Likelihood Moderate if Raiders struggle Latest Insider Update Cowboys previously made three offers for Crosby Potential Return George Pickens, multiple draft picks and additional assets

Which teams are interested in Maxx Crosby?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

The Dallas Cowboys are once again at the center of blockbuster trade speculation, this time in a proposal that would send $96 million Las Vegas Raiders pass rusher Maxx Crosby to Dallas while moving wide receiver George Pickens to the Tennessee Titans. Although nothing is imminent, the idea has gained attention after a new report connected the Cowboys to a complex three-team deal that would reshape all three rosters before the 2026 season.The Cowboys remain the team most closely linked with Crosby after their long-standing interest resurfaced in recent trade discussions. Dallas has been searching for another elite edge rusher to pair with Micah Parsons, believing the combination could create one of the NFL's most dangerous pass-rushing units.The latest proposal, outlined by Bleacher Report's Kristopher Knox, would send Crosby and wide receiver Calvin Ridley to Dallas. In return, George Pickens would head to the Titans, while Las Vegas would receive Pickens, multiple draft selections and rookie receiver Elic Ayomanor.For Tennessee, adding Pickens would give second-year quarterback Cam Ward another proven target alongside Wan'Dale Robinson and rookie Carnell Tate.That combination could strengthen the Titans' long-term offensive outlook while giving Pickens the multi-year opportunity he has reportedly been seeking.Knox believes Dallas has enough motivation to revisit talks after previous attempts to acquire Crosby fell short.“According to ESPN's Ryan McFadden, the Dallas Cowboys made three separate offers for (Maxx) Crosby, though none were enough to elicit action from the Raiders,” Knox wrote Wednesday.He added:“In this deal (Crosby and Calvin Ridley to the Cowboys, Pickens to the Titans, and a 2027 Cowboys first-round pick, a Cowboys 2027 fifth-round pick, a Titans’ 2027 second-round pick, and Elic Ayomanor to the Raiders), the Cowboys would cough up first- and fifth-round picks, along with wide receiver George Pickens.”Knox also noted:“Pickens had a spectacular 2025 campaign in Dallas but is now set to play on the franchise tag and isn't expected to receive an extension from the Cowboys.""So, Dallas would flip Pickens to the Titans, who would pair him with Wan'Dale Robinson and rookie Carnell Tate to give second-year quarterback Cam Ward a strong long-term receiving trio.”Crosby remains under a lucrative four-year, $96 million contract, making any trade financially significant for both teams. There has been no indication that he has a no-trade clause, meaning the Raiders would have flexibility if they chose to begin a roster reset.Pickens, meanwhile, is expected to play on the franchise tag after failing to secure a long-term extension with Dallas. That uncertainty has fueled speculation that the Cowboys could consider moving him if the right offer becomes available instead of committing major money to another wide receiver.For Dallas, landing Crosby would immediately strengthen a defense already built around an aggressive pass rush. Pairing him with Parsons could create constant pressure on opposing quarterbacks and improve the Cowboys' chances of competing for the NFC title.The Raiders would gain valuable draft capital and a productive young receiver if they decide to build for the future, while the Titans would address a major offensive need by giving Ward another established playmaker. For now, however, the proposal remains hypothetical, and any serious negotiations are likely to depend on how Las Vegas opens the 2026 season.
Article Summary: The Dallas Cowboys have been linked to a proposed three-team trade that would bring a $96 million Raiders star to Dallas. The idea, floated by Bleacher Report's Kristopher Knox, follows reports that the Cowboys previously made multiple offers for the Raiders standout. While the trade remains hypothetical, Dallas' long-standing interest and Pickens' uncertain contract situation continue to fuel speculation.

Article Title: “This is beyond disappointing”: Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video raises eyebrows while kidnapping case takes dramatic turn
Article Text:
Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video raises eyebrows while kidnapping case takes dramatic turn(Getty)

Terrion Arnold’s leaked court video sparks reactions while details of the Tampa incident stay in focus

Terrion Arnold’s kidnapping case grows more serious as co-defendants flip and the bond gets denied



Terrion Arnold’s arrest has quickly become one of the biggest NFL stories of the offseason. The Detroit Lions cornerback surrendered to Florida authorities after being charged in connection with a February kidnapping and armed robbery case in Tampa, putting both his freedom and football future in serious doubt.Now, Terrion Arnold’s court appearance has pushed the case into a new spotlight after leaked courtroom footage sparked strong reactions online, while fresh legal developments gave prosecutors even more momentum ahead of Monday’s hearing.The leaked courtroom video became the first thing people noticed Thursday. Terrion Arnold stood before a Hillsborough County judge wearing a green anti-suicide safety vest, often used inside county jails for inmates under closer supervision. That visual quickly became the center of online discussion.Social media is filled with questions. One fan asked, “Why did they get him in a turtle suit?” Another wrote, “This got to be a ritual.”Another said, “Damn, suicide watch!?!? They have camp in like 5 weeks!!!!”Another added, “It’s a Detroit thing 😭 toxic out here.”One folk added, “This is beyond disappointing on all levels. Gotta be careful with folks you deal with”.According to investigators, the Terrion Arnold arrest connects back to a February incident in Tampa. Prosecutors allege Arnold helped orchestrate a plan where three men were lured into an apartment, then robbed at gunpoint and held against their will. Authorities later filed four kidnapping charges and four armed robbery charges against him, making this one of the most serious criminal cases involving an active NFL player this year.For many following the case, the leaked video only pushed more attention toward an investigation that is still unfolding.Terrion Arnold’s first court hearing brought no relief. The judge denied bond on June 25 and ordered him to remain in custody through the weekend. His next court appearance is set for Monday, where his legal team will again argue for release.But what makes that hearing even more important is what happened earlier the same day. Two co-defendants tied to the same case pleaded guilty in Hillsborough County and agreed to cooperate with prosecutors. Both are now expected to testify against Terrion Arnold, a major shift that strengthens the state’s case.That development has already changed public reaction. One user wrote, “His circle is trash,” blaming the people around Arnold for dragging him deeper into the case. Another comment suggested Arnold’s friends may have turned on him to reduce their own sentences.Arnold’s defence team is pushing back hard. His agent, Denise White of EAG Sports Management, released a statement saying Terrion Arnold “categorically denies” any involvement and insisted prosecutors are relying on convicted felons trying to cut deals.Support has also come from outside his camp. Sauce Gardner publicly defended Arnold, calling him a “good kid” and saying the allegations do not match the person he knows.For the Detroit Lions, this situation creates major uncertainty. Terrion Arnold was expected to be a key part of Detroit’s secondary this season. Now, with the Terrion Arnold arrest case moving quickly and witness testimony building, Monday’s hearing could become the biggest turning point yet.
Article Summary: Terrion Arnold’s kidnapping case grows more serious as co-defendants flip and the bond gets denied. The Detroit Lions cornerback surrendered to Florida authorities after being charged in connection with a February kidnapping and armed robbery case in Tampa. The leaked courtroom video became the first thing people noticed Thursday.

Article Title: NBA insider drops bad news on longest tenured Knicks star weeks after championship victory
Article Text:
Mitchell Robinson. Image via: Jesse D. Garrabrant /NBAE via Getty Images

NBA insider reveals Mitchell Robinson's future with the Knicks



Why the Knicks may struggle to keep their championship core together



Mitchell Robinson. Image via: Emilee Chinn/Getty Images

The New York Knicks could be headed for a difficult offseason decision after an NBA insider cast fresh doubt over Mitchell Robinson's future with the franchise. Just weeks after winning the 2026 NBA championship, multiple reports now suggest the Knicks are unlikely to retain their longest-tenured player because of looming salary cap restrictions.The uncertainty centers on New York's determination to remain below the NBA's second luxury tax apron. That financial approach could force the front office to break up key pieces of a championship roster despite Robinson's importance on both ends of the floor.Mitchell Robinson enters free agency after completing the four-year, $60 million contract he signed in 2022. While appearing on ESPN's Unsportsmanlike radio show, Brian Windhorst said Robinson's return remains far from certain.“He’s a bit of a polarizing player because he’s had some health issues and he hasn’t been able to play starter’s minutes, so do you give him starter money?" Windhorst said.“So, I would say Mitch Robinson is 50/50. I don’t know, maybe 51-49 to come back. But that’s open that he might not be there.”That assessment followed SNY's Ian Begley reporting there are "several teams with big-time interest" in Robinson, with at least one organization potentially offering him a starting role.Veteran Knicks reporter Stefan Bondy echoed the growing expectation, posting, "Heard it’s unlikely Mitchell Robinson will be back with Knicks next season."The combination of outside interest and New York's financial limits has turned Robinson into one of the franchise's biggest offseason questions.The Knicks entered the draft focused on creating financial flexibility, but keeping Robinson may still prove difficult.ESPN's Shams Charania reported that Robinson and guard Landry Shamet are not expected to return as unrestricted free agents because owner James Dolan is unwilling to exceed the second apron. Salary cap projections indicate New York has limited room to re-sign both players while filling out the remainder of its roster.Robinson's value further complicates negotiations. Although injuries have prevented him from consistently handling starter-level minutes, he remains one of the NBA's premier offensive rebounders. Last season, he posted a 23.9 percent offensive rebounding rate, surpassing the previous league record held by Steven Adams.Analysts Fred Katz and Sam Vecenie recently projected Robinson could command roughly two years and $30 million on the open market, while estimates around the league suggest the Knicks are unlikely to exceed $15 million annually if they remain committed to staying below the second apron.Robinson averaged 4.8 points and 5.9 rebounds in 14 minutes per game during the championship season after the Knicks carefully managed his workload throughout the year. His rim protection and rebounding became critical during the playoff run, making any departure difficult to replace.New York now faces a defining offseason choice. It can preserve financial flexibility or spend beyond the second apron to retain one of the longest-serving and most respected players in the organization, a decision that could reshape the roster before the franchise begins defending its NBA title.
Article Summary: NBA insider casts fresh doubt over Mitchell Robinson's future with the New York Knicks. Multiple reports suggest the Knicks are unlikely to retain their longest-tenured player because of looming salary cap restrictions. The uncertainty centers on New York's determination to remain below the NBA's second luxury tax apron.

Article Title: Doug Martin cause of death: Fresh lawsuit reveals troubling new details about former NFL star’s final hours
Article Text:
Doug Martin cause of death: Fresh lawsuit reveals troubling new details about former NFL star’s final hours (Image via Getty)

Doug Martin's cause of death: Ex-NFL star's parents claim police restraint played a role in his death

Doug Martin’s family wants answers as his football legacy meets a painful final chapter

Former NFL running back Doug Martin’s death shocked the football world when news broke that he had died at 36 last October. Now, a fresh lawsuit has brought troubling new details into focus as Doug Martin’s parents are pushing for answers about his final moments.Recent reports say the lawsuit targets the City of Oakland, the Oakland Police Department, and ambulance provider Falck Northern California. The family claims Doug Martin’s final moments involved police restraint and delayed emergency care during what they describe as a mental health crisis.According to recent reports, Doug Martin’s mother called for medical help after the former Tampa Bay Buccaneers star began struggling during a mental health episode at home. Before paramedics arrived, Doug Martin reportedly left the house and entered a nearby home, where police later found him.That encounter now sits at the center of the lawsuit. The complaint says officers restrained Doug Martin face down while pressing on his back. The family claims that after officers turned him over, Doug Martin showed no response. Reports say officers initially believed he was pretending to sleep before realizing the situation had turned critical.Per Associated Press, the lawsuit also alleges Doug Martin did not receive timely medical care after becoming unresponsive. His parents claim paramedics arrived more than 15 minutes after officers requested emergency assistance and did not begin treatment immediately after reaching the scene. Alongside the police restraint allegations, the family argues that the delayed medical response was a major factor in Doug Martin’s death.Family attorney John Burris told The Athletic, while discussing the lawsuit, “They just want to know what happened.”He also added, “When you call for help, and the police come, it’s not a death warrant.”Those remarks now sit at the heart of this legal fight. The family believes Doug Martin died from restraint asphyxia, which means pressure during the restraint may have cut off his breathing. An independent pathologist reportedly shared that early opinion, though the official findings are still pending.The lawsuit is not just about damages. Doug Martin’s parents are also looking for the full autopsy and toxicology reports, which have still not been released. Recent reports say the coroner’s investigation remains open.At the same time, Doug Martin’s mother has sent his brain to the Boston University CTE Center for testing. The family wants to know if repeated football hits played a role in his mental state before his death.Doug Martin built a strong NFL career after the Tampa Bay Buccaneers drafted him in the first round. He rushed for over 5,300 yards, scored 30 touchdowns, made two Pro Bowls, and earned First-Team All-Pro honors. Doug Martin also spent his final NFL season with the Las Vegas Raiders.For NFL fans, Martin was one of the league’s most explosive backs at his peak. But right now, his family’s fight has shifted the focus away from football and toward the unanswered questions surrounding Doug Martin’s death.
Article Summary: Doug Martin’s parents have filed a lawsuit against the City of Oakland, the Oakland Police Department, and ambulance provider Falck Northern California. The family claims police restraint and delayed emergency care played a role in the death of the former NFL running back. Doug Martin died at the age of 36 last October.

Article Title: “She should be banned indefinitely”: Furious Caitlin Clark fans slam WNBA’s soft punishment to Alyssa Thomas for hitting Fever star’s throat
Article Text:
Alyssa Thomas tries to regain her footing over Caitlin Clark. Image via: Grace Smith / IndyStar / USA Today Network via Imagn

WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas one game after hit to Caitlin Clark's throat



Caitlin Clark. Image via: Grace Smith/IndyStar / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images

Fever fans are fuming over WNBA's soft punishment of Alyssa Thomas for hitting Caitlin Clark's throat



What Fever coach Stephanie White said about the incident



WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas one game after hit to Caitlin Clark's throat, but the league's disciplinary decision has done little to quiet the debate surrounding player safety. The WNBA announced Thursday that Thomas received a retroactive Flagrant Foul 2 and a one-game suspension after making contact with Clark's throat during Phoenix's 111-109 victory over the Indiana Fever.The punishment answers one question about the league's response, but it has created another. Many Fever supporters believe the suspension is too lenient after officials failed to call a foul during the game, allowing a controversial sequence to unfold without an immediate penalty.The play occurred with 6:52 remaining in the second quarter as Clark and Thomas both dove for a loose ball. Video review showed Thomas making contact with her fist to Clark's throat in what the league later ruled was a non-basketball act.No whistle was blown during live action. Following its review, the WNBA upgraded the play and announced disciplinary action."Per WNBA rule, the League Office has the option, following its review of any game, to reclassify a Flagrant foul or to classify as Flagrant any foul not called as such during a game and may impose a fine and/or suspension," the league said in its statement.Thomas will serve the suspension Saturday when the Mercury visit the Toronto Tempo.The incident came during an increasingly physical series between Phoenix and Indiana. Clark later exited with a back injury after another sequence in the game and finished with 19 points and eight assists in 20 minutes. The Fever ultimately lost 111-109.The league's decision immediately sparked strong reactions online, with many fans arguing that one game failed to match the seriousness of the play.One fan wrote, "One game. That’s it 😡. She should be banned indefinitely."Another added, "Hell of a precedent set with only a 1 game suspension," while a third posted, "What a joke. One game. Needs to kicked out of league."Some responses went even further. "Thomas should be expelled from the WNBA!" one user wrote, while another commented, "That’s weak. She should be investigated for assault. It’s clear as day assault with intent."Others took a more measured view. One fan wrote, "Should be longer, but some action taken is better than no action taken."The backlash followed days of heightened discussion surrounding Clark after another heated matchup between the Fever and Mercury, fueling continued conversations about consistency in officiating and player protection.Indiana coach Stephanie White was outspoken after the game, criticizing both the missed call and the standard of officiating.“It was egregious. The fact that it was a no-call … You got to call it,” White said. “You’re coming in here aware of what happened two nights ago and that (expletive) still happens? Absolutely unacceptable.”She later expanded on her frustration.“Absolutely disrespectful. We have a generational talent and a WNBA superstar who had two cheap shots right there that weren’t called," she said. "It's absolutely egregious, and utterly disrespectful. [Clark] is not called the same way as everybody else is called. The fist in the throat is crazy. It's crazy. It's dangerous."The Fever also reinforced that message through team president Kelly Krauskopf.“Player safety should be paramount in our league,” Krauskopf said. “We appreciate the WNBA’s review of last night’s incident and the action taken. Right now our focus is on Caitlin and our entire team as we prepare for Saturday.”With Thomas sidelined for Phoenix's next game and Clark's status still under close watch following her back issue, scrutiny of how the league handles physical play is unlikely to fade anytime soon.
Article Summary: Alyssa Thomas received a retroactive Flagrant Foul 2 and a one-game suspension. Thomas made contact with her fist to Caitlin Clark's throat during Phoenix's 111-109 victory over the Indiana Fever. The league's decision sparked strong reactions online, with many fans arguing that one game failed to match the seriousness of the play.

Article Title: ESPN's Matt Miller reveals emotional health update after surviving horrific crash that led to life-saving arm amputation
Article Text:
ESPN Analyst Matt Miller (Getty Images)

What happened to Matt Miller and how serious are his injuries?

Has a GoFundMe been started for Matt Miller?

ESPN NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller is fighting back after a devastating car accident in Missouri left him with life-threatening injuries, including the amputation of his left arm. Miller, 42, was airlifted to Mercy Hospital following the crash on June 17, where his Ford Bronco crossed into oncoming traffic and struck a semi-truck head-on. He publicly shared his condition on June 23, and on Friday underwent surgery to determine whether his shoulder can support a prosthetic arm.The crash happened around 4 p.m. on Highway 71 in Jasper County. According to a police report obtained by local Fox affiliate KOAM, Matt Miller's 2023 Ford Bronco crossed the centre line and hit the driver's side of a semi-truck. The truck driver tried to swerve but both vehicles ended up off the south side of the road.Miller was airlifted to Mercy Hospital, where doctors performed emergency surgery. He lost his left arm in what he described as a life-saving amputation. "I sustained significant injuries, including multiple fractures and broken ribs," he wrote on X. "I also underwent a life-saving amputation of my left arm. While I have a long road ahead, I'm focused on my recovery and taking things one day at a time."On Friday, Miller posted an update ahead of another procedure. "Today's surgery is a big one," he wrote. "To find out if my shoulder can be reconstructed in order to support a prosthetic arm. Your continued thoughts and prayers are so appreciated."Despite everything, Miller has already told people he plans to get back to ESPN for the 2027 NFL Draft cycle. "I look forward to continuing my recovery and getting back to ESPN to talk football," he said.His ESPN colleagues responded quickly. Adam Schefter posted, "Best wishes for a speedy recovery. We are pulling for you!" Mina Kimes added, "Ah, Matt I'm so sorry to hear this and so relieved to hear you pulled through. We're all behind you."Yes. The Miller family launched a GoFundMe on June 25 to help cover the mountain of costs that come with an injury this severe. As of this writing, more than $37,000 has already been raised."Matt remains in the ICU, where he continues to make inspiring progress each day," his family wrote on the page. "The months ahead will include extensive medical care, rehabilitation, travel to appointments, prosthetic planning, and countless unexpected expenses."His family also made sure to note that, in typical fashion, Miller has not lost his sense of humour. He's apparently already joking that he plans to become "the best one-armed bartender ever."The GoFundMe is open to donations of any size, and the family says even sharing the page makes a difference.
Article Summary: ESPN NFL Draft analyst Matt Miller is fighting back after a devastating car accident. Miller, 42, was airlifted to Mercy Hospital following the crash on June 17. He publicly shared his condition on June 23, and on Friday underwent surgery to determine whether his shoulder can support a prosthetic arm.

Article Title: "Show me a picture of them kissing": Two-time Super Bowl champ rejects Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel affair claims
Article Text:
Dianna Russini. (Getty Images)

Why is LeSean McCoy rejecting the allegations against Dianna Russini?

The Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel controversy refuses to die down. New York Times published a detailed report this week revealing that Russini was earning $800,000 annually at The Athletic before resigning in April, days after Page Six broke the first pictures of her and the New England Patriots head coach holding hands at an adults-only resort in Arizona. The Athletic has separately confirmed its investigation into Russini's journalistic conduct remains active.On Wednesday's episode of the Speakeasy podcast, two-time Super Bowl champion LeSean McCoy dismissed the Dianna Russini affair allegations outright, pointing to what he called a complete absence of concrete proof."I read what she said when she was resigning, and I think her integrity and her respect for her and her family is bigger than some money," McCoy said. "That's why she left, because they were dragging her family in it, her kids in it, and her husband in it — all because she wanted to hang out with a friend of hers. Ya'll want to play, but there hasn't been no true facts, for real. Some pictures that look [a certain way], a video that stopped and froze. I don't do all that."McCoy wasn't done. He went after the media's appetite for scandal, arguing that slowed-down footage and frozen frames don't constitute evidence of anything."I live in the gray area, and I know what's really going on. The media loves negativity. The media wants to talk bad about you. The media want to talk bad about you. They don't want to say anything good about her [or] about him. So, they slow a video down. 'Oh, look, they're kissing!' Unless I see a real video of him kissing her, of him grabbing her, of him doing something to her, I don't believe that. I don't believe that. Show me a picture of them kissing; you don't see no lip-on-lip action. "Former NFL linebacker Emmanuel Acho pushed back hard. His argument wasn't about rumour or innuendo. It was about the gaps in Dianna Russini's own defence."Dianna Russini resigns saying like, 'This is all a lie. This is all fabrication. Mike and I are just friends. We were there with six individuals. All of this is a lie,'" Acho said. "And The Athletic was like, 'Well, Dianna, where were the six individuals? Just prove them. We can't find them. Our investigation doesn't turn up any six friends.'"Acho then listed what he described as a mounting trail of circumstantial evidence."Now, there are more pictures about you and Mike Vrabel," he added. "There's a playlist of you and Mike Vrabel. There's a picture of you seven months pregnant on a boat with Mike Vrabel. You named your son Mike. There's too much evidence and empirical data that's going against you. So, that to me is why, like, she tried to play in everybody's face. I feel bad because an $800,000 job is still an $800,000 job. And in theory, Mike Vrabel really faced no occupational consequences besides missing a day of work. "That last point lands with some weight. Vrabel skipped Day 3 of the 2026 NFL Draft while addressing the fallout with his family, and he later admitted his "previous actions don't meet the standard that I hold myself to." He has not confirmed an affair, but the cryptic phrasing has fuelled more speculation than silence would have. The boating trip photographs from 2021, when Russini was pregnant and Vrabel was still coaching the Tennessee Titans, have only deepened the questions.McCoy and Acho are looking at the same set of facts and landing in entirely different places. What's clear is that neither Russini nor Vrabel has fully closed the door on this story.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini resigned from her $800,000-a-year job at The Athletic in April. She was accused of having an affair with New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. LeSean McCoy says there is no evidence of an affair between the two.

Article Title: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes celebrates major KC Current milestone as Chiefs return inches closer
